rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LEUNG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notrice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate. The petition for writ of mandate is denied. The admission of the Cantamount testimony and invoice (Exhibit 29) does not mandate reversal of respondent's decision. In Thornbrough v. W. Placer Unified Sch. Dist. (2013) 223 Cal.App.4th 169, 200, the court explains: " 'Error of law is not reversible unless, on an examination of the record, it appears to have resulted in a miscarriage of justice.' " [citation] And it is well-settled that the improper admission or rejection of evidence at an administrative hearing does not provide "grounds for reversal unless the error has resulted in a miscarriage of justice. [Citation.] In other words, it must be reasonably probable a more favorable result would have been reached absent the error." The Wilson violations alone (specifications 4 and 5) support the termination of petitioner. The record demonstrates that petitioner illegally accessed CLETS in 2005 and was guilty of the same violation with regard to the Wilson brothers several years later. Respondent did not abuse its discretion in rejecting petitioner's claim that he was performing a proper police investigation. It is noteworthy that petitioner never contacted the Gang Task Force, which was the body responsible for such an investigation. In addition, the record showed that Officer Lozada contacted Hayward Police and attempted to obtain information regarding the Wilsons, but the Hayward officer did not provide any information because he was unsure regarding Officer Lozada's status. It is reasonable to assume that Officer Lozada probably engaged in the same type of conduct with petitioner. The court does not find petitioner's other arguments persuasive. While the commissioners are supposed to affirm on the record that they reviewed all of the evidence at the guilt phase (Rule XII (B)(4)), there is no reason to believe that the commissioners actually failed to review all of the evidence. There is no support for petitioner's argument that the same commissioners need to preside over the guilt and penalty phases. The commission rules do not support that assertion, respondent's interpretation of its own rules is entitled to judicial deference, and such an interpretation rule would be unworkable given the turnover at the Police Commission and other circumstances that may lead to a commissioner being unavailable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TOM VS. JEREMY D BLANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With This Court'S Order Of March 31, 2014; Request For Terminating And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4620.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK Motion To Compel Compliance With This Court's Order Of March 31, 2014; Request For Terminating And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4620.00. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Judge Pro Tem Woodson has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Parties to meet and confer prior to the hearing, and inform the Court of any remaining discovery issues pursuant to prior Court orders of March 31, 2014 and July 9, 2014. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CAL INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. KIEWIT INFRASTRUCTURE WEST CO., A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Element 26 Environmental Demoltion, Inc. For Summary Judgment Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication, Separate Statement Of Undisputed Facts, Supporting Memorandum And Supporting Evidence And Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANTs ELEMENT 26 ENVIRONMENTAL & DEMOLITION, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication. The Motion is denied in its entirety. First, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether there is an unpaid balance owed to Element 26 for the Crystal Springs project. Pursuant to the Payment Bond, payment is due only for work provided for in the subcontract and for other authorized additions to and modifications of that subcontract. (Bryant Dec. Ex. HH.) Whether all of the work performed and billed by Element 26 on the Crystal Springs project was in conformance with the project specifications, and whether Element 26 was fully paid for the properly billed and approved work that it provided are material facts that are in dispute. (UMF # 168, 173.) Second, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Element was underpaid for work that it performed on the Harry Tracy project. (UMF # 138.) Additionally, there is a triable issue as to whether Element 26 was acting as a subcontractor on the project, and what the terms of the contract were. (UMF # 114, 131, 138.) In light of the existence of these triable issues, Element 26 has failed to show that it is entitled to judgment on any cause of action against any party. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ARMSTRONG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. There exist triable issues of material fact regarding (1) where the IV line was inserting into plaintiff's wrist; (2) and whether defendant breached the standard of care and caused injury to plaintiff. (See Plaintiff's deposition, pgs. 145-148 and declarations of Dr. Timothy Angelotti and Dr. Tracy Newkirk). The court overrules defendant's evidentiary objections. rulings.Case Title: MAYA DI NAPOLI VS. FOUR SEASONS HOTELS LIMITED, A CANADIAN CORPORATIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Elletranove/Jeff Capaccio
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014, Line 3, Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Elletranove/Jeff Capaccio. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Judge Pro Tem Woodson has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant ANNA GATTI's Motion to Quash is GRANTED, in part. Items 1 and 2 of the deposition subpoena issued to the Custodian of Records for Elletranova/Jeff Capaccio on August 14, 2014 are within the scope of discovery with regard to the allegations stated in Plaintiff MAYA DI NAPOLI's Complaint, and shall be produced. Item 3 of the deposition subpoena issued to Defendant on August 14, 2014 is overbroad, and is hereby QUASHED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
